title: else and finally
subtitle: Run code when things go right, and always clean up
learning_objectives: Use the else clause to run code only when no exception occurred
Use finally to run cleanup code that always executes
Apply finally to ensure files are always closed

sections.heading: The else Clause
sections.content: A try block can have an else clause. The else block runs only if no exception occurred. It is the success path. This is cleaner than putting success code inside the try block, because it keeps the try block small.

sections.heading: The finally Clause
sections.content: The finally block always runs — whether an exception occurred or not. It is used for cleanup tasks: closing files, releasing resources, printing a summary. You cannot skip a finally block.
sections.code_output:
Result: 5.0
This always runs, no matter what.
---
Division error!
Cleanup complete.

exercises.title: Use else for Success
exercises.prompt: Write code that tries to convert "100" to an integer. If it fails (ValueError), print 'Conversion failed'. If it succeeds (else), print 'Got the number: 100'.
hints: Add an else: block after the except block
The else block runs only when no exception occurred
Print 'Got the number:' followed by value

exercises.title: Always Run Cleanup with finally
exercises.prompt: Write a try/except/finally block. In try: do 10 / 0. In except ZeroDivisionError: print 'Caught division error'. In finally: print 'Finished.'.
hints: Add: except ZeroDivisionError: print("Caught division error")

quiz.question: When does the 'else' block in a try/except/else run?
options: Only when an exception was raised
Only when NO exception was raised
quiz.explanation: The else block is the 'success' path — it only runs when the try block completed without raising any exception.

quiz.question: When does the 'finally' block run?
options: Only when an exception was raised
Only when no exception was raised
Always, whether or not an exception occurred
Only when the else block also runs
quiz.explanation: finally always runs — it is the cleanup block. Whether the try succeeded, failed, or the except block ran, finally will always execute. This is perfect for closing files or releasing resources.

completion_message: Excellent! You now know the full try/except/else/finally pattern!
